duplication_percentage.name: Duplication Percentage
duplication_percentage.description: Fraction of total source tokens that appear in at least one clone group. Computed over the full analyzed file set.

token_count.name: Token Count
token_count.description: Number of normalized source tokens in the clone group. Tokens are language-aware (keywords, identifiers, operators, punctuation). Higher token count = larger duplicate.
token_count.interpretation: larger clones have higher refactoring value

line_count.name: Line Count
line_count.description: Number of source lines spanned by the clone instance. Approximation of clone size for human readability.
line_count.interpretation: larger clones are more impactful to deduplicate

clone_groups.name: Clone Groups
clone_groups.description: A set of code fragments with identical or near-identical normalized token sequences. Each group has 2+ instances across different locations.
clone_groups.interpretation: each group is a single refactoring opportunity

clone_families.name: Clone Families
clone_families.description: Groups of clone groups that share the same set of files. Indicates systematic duplication patterns (e.g., mirrored directory structures).
